info.title: Timbal Platform API
info.description: Public API documentation for the Timbal platform

get.description: List jobs for an ACE, most recent first.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return (clamped to 200). Defaults to 50.

get.description: Fetch a single job. Poll this until `status` reaches a terminal value (`succeeded`, `failed`, `cancelled`, `error`).

post.description: Cancel an active job. The job transitions to `status = 'cancelled'` once the cancellation takes effect. Returns `204` on accept; `409` if the job is not currently active.

Re-enqueue a failed/cancelled/errored job with the same kind and input, returning a new `job_uid`. Returns `409` if the source job is still active or already succeeded, or if another job is active on this ACE.

409.description: Source job not retriable, or ACE has a live job

get.description: List the policies declared on an ACE.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

post.description: OpenAI-compatible **Chat Completions** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` request body; set `stream: true` for an SSE response. The upstream provider is resolved from the `model` field: either explicit (`anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, `google/gemini-2.5-pro`, …) or best-effort from the model name prefix (`gpt-*`, `claude-*`, `gemini-*`, `grok-*`); otherwise falls back to OpenAI. Supported providers: `openai`, `google`, `togetherai`, `xai`, `groq`, `fireworks`, `xiaomi`, `byteplus`. Request body remains OpenAI-shaped regardless of provider. Authenticate with a Timbal API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

post.description: Anthropic-compatible **Messages** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native Anthropic `/v1/messages` request body; set `stream: true` for an SSE response. The `model` field accepts either a bare model name (`claude-opus-4-7`) or an explicit `anthropic/<model>` prefix; any other provider prefix is rejected with `400`. Authenticate with a Timbal API key passed in the `x-api-key` header (same header the Anthropic SDK sends). Optional `anthropic-version` and `anthropic-beta` headers are forwarded upstream. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

post.description: OpenAI-compatible **Responses API** endpoint scoped to an ACE. Accepts the native OpenAI `/v1/responses` request body; streaming responses are delivered as SSE. The upstream provider is resolved from the `model` field: either explicit (`google/gemini-2.5-pro`, `xai/grok-3`, …) or best-effort from the model name prefix (`gpt-*`, `gemini-*`, `grok-*`); otherwise falls back to OpenAI. Supported providers: `openai`, `google`, `togetherai`, `xai`, `groq`, `fireworks`. Request body remains OpenAI-shaped regardless of provider. Authenticate with a Timbal API key via `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Usage is billed to the organization that owns the ACE.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

200.description: Upstream response (JSON or SSE depending on `stream`)

get.description: List the variables declared on an ACE.

parameters.name: ace_uid
parameters.description: ACE uid (UUID).

post.description: Upload a short-lived file (auto-deleted ~24h after upload).

file.description: Binary contents of the file. Max 100 MB.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

patch.description: Patch the authenticated user's profile. Omitted fields are left untouched; null clears nullable fields.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

400.description: Validation failed or non-nullable field set to null

get.description: List organizations the authenticated user belongs to

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional search query to filter orgs by name

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Page token

get.description: List the authenticated user's API credentials.

post.description: Create an API credential for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Revoke an API credential owned by the authenticated user.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

get.description: Get an organization by ID
Read credit budgets for the organization. Filter with `project_id` or `role_id` to fetch a single target without listing the full org config.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: When set, return at most the project budget for this project (0 or 1 rows).

parameters.name: role_id
parameters.description: When set, return at most the role budget for this role (0 or 1 rows).

404.description: Filtered project or role not found

put.description: Replace the organization's full credit budget configuration. Omitted targets are removed.

patch.description: Merge credit budget changes. Each row upserts when `limit_credits` is set, or removes the budget when `limit_credits` is null. Other configured targets are left unchanged.

post.description: Enable or disable usage-based overage billing and set an optional credit cap for the organization.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

403.description: Caller cannot manage billing for this organization

422.description: Cannot apply overage (plan, payments, or subscription state)

parameters.name: path

parameters.name: path

parameters.name: path

get.description: Account-side contacts assigned to an organization.
Credit grants awarded to an organization, most recent first.
List connected domains for an organization

post.description: Add a custom domain to an organization

get.description: Get a domain by ID. This endpoint triggers DNS validation and returns the live certificate status.

delete.description: Delete a domain

get.description: List all available embedding models for the organization

Catalog of every action this organization may grant on a role. Org-scoped so future plans / feature flags can enable subsets per-org; today every org sees the full catalog. Use to drive the custom-role builder UI: render a checkbox per action, and a resource picker keyed on `resource.kind` for typed actions. Cacheable per `(org_id, version)`.

200.description: Action catalog visible to this org

403.description: Caller is not a member of this org

post.description:
Forward authorization simulator. Evaluates one or more `(action, resource?)` checks against a user's effective grants and returns a decision per check, including the matched allow/deny grants that explain it. Mirrors the live evaluator exactly — same scope intersection, expiry filtering, and deny-beats-allow precedence. 

Defaults `user_id` to the caller; passing another user requires `users.read`. Cap is 50 checks per request.

403.description: Forbidden (not a member, or asking about another user without `users.read`)

422.description: Invalid request (empty/too many checks, unknown action key, malformed or wildcarded resource, target user is not a member)

get.description: List roles available to an organization. Returns built-in system roles + any custom roles defined by the org.

post.description: Create a custom role for the organization.

409.description: A role with that name already exists in the organization

422.description: Invalid request body (unknown action, bad resource shape, reserved name, duplicate grant), or plan does not allow custom roles (`code: PLAN_REQUIRED`)

get.description: Retrieve a role and the full set of grants it carries.

delete.description: Delete a custom role. System roles cannot be deleted. Roles still attached to members return 409 with the assignee list — reassign first, then retry.

409.description: Role is still attached to one or more members

422.description: Deleting this role would lock the caller out of roles.manage, or plan does not allow custom roles (`code: PLAN_REQUIRED`)

patch.description: Partially update a custom role. System roles are immutable.

409.description: A role with that name already exists

422.description: Invalid body, would lock the caller out of roles.manage, or plan does not allow custom roles (`code: PLAN_REQUIRED`)

get.description: List members of an organization. Requires `users.read`.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter on user name or email.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description:
Page token for pagination. Pass back the `next_page_token` from the
previous response.

post.description: Invite one or more users to an organization

delete.description: Remove a user from an organization

get.description: Dump the grants a user actually carries in this org, post-scope-intersection and expiry-filtered, with role attribution. Asking about yourself only requires membership; asking about another user requires `users.read`. Same effective grant contributed by N roles produces N rows.

403.description: Forbidden (not a member, or asking about another user without `users.read`)

422.description: Target user is not a member of this organization

put.description: Replace a member's role attachments with the provided set. Each attachment is a `(role_id, scope?)` pair — the same role may appear multiple times scoped to different resources. Requires `users.edit`.

get.description: List knowledge bases in an organization

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Page token for pagination

post.description: Create a new knowledge base

get.description: Get knowledge base details

delete.description: Delete a knowledge base

get.description: List backups for a knowledge base.

post.description: Create an on-demand backup of a knowledge base. While running, all read/write traffic on the KB returns 503 (Maintenance). A mutually-exclusive lock prevents concurrent backup/restore on the same KB (409 Conflict). `dry_run = true` skips the lock and the upload.

201.description: Backup accepted; KB is already paused for maintenance (non-dry-run). Poll via GET /backups/{id}.

409.description: KB is already under maintenance (no backup row created)

get.description: Get a single backup.

post.description: Create a virtual directory in the knowledge base

409.description: A file with the same name already exists at this path

get.description: List files and folders in a knowledge base (use directory=/ for root)

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Page token for pagination

parameters.name: directory
parameters.description:
Scope listing to one folder. Use `"/"` for KB root (entries with no parent path).
Omit to list every file in the knowledge base. Otherwise a normalized path such as `docs/reports`.

post.description: Add a file to a knowledge base via direct upload or remote URL fetch. Provide exactly one of `file` (raw bytes) or `url` (http/https; the server fetches it with an SSRF-hardened client).

schema.description:
Request body for adding a file to a knowledge base.

Provide **either** `file` (raw bytes via multipart) **or** `url` (http/https URL
the server fetches directly). Exactly one is required.

directory.description: Optional subdirectory path to organize the file within the knowledge base.

file.description: File bytes (multipart `file` field). Mutually exclusive with `url`.

metadata.description: Optional JSON metadata to attach to the file.

parse.description:
Per-upload override for the parse/chunk/embed pipeline. Accepts
`"true"`/`"false"` (or `"1"`/`"0"`). When omitted, the knowledge base's
auto-processing setting and the file extension decide. `true` forces
parsing even when the KB has auto-processing off (still credit-gated;
unsupported extensions silently fall back to stored-as-is). `false`
stores the file as-is and never runs the pipeline.

url.description:
Public http/https URL the server fetches and ingests directly.
SSRF-hardened (internal hosts blocked). Mutually exclusive with `file`.

RateLimit-Limit.description: Max requests per window for this user.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

409.description: A file with the same name already exists in this directory

RateLimit-Limit.description: Max requests per window for this user.

RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests left in the current window.

RateLimit-Reset.description: Seconds until the current window resets.

Retry-After.description: Seconds the client should wait before retrying.

get.description: Get a file with its parsings and embeddings metadata

200.description: File details with parsings and embeddings

delete.description: Delete a file from a knowledge base

get.description: List chunks for a knowledge base file, in document order. Layout metadata (`blocks`) is omitted from each item to keep responses small; fetch a single chunk by `uid` to retrieve it.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description:
Cursor: pass `next_page_token` from the previous response to fetch
the next page. Treat the value as opaque.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 100; max 500.

post.description: Insert a new chunk. Place via `after_uid` / `before_uid` (mutually exclusive) or omit both to append.

201.description: Chunk inserted. Search results reflect the change after re-indexing completes (`index_status` field).

409.description: No insertion slot available between the specified neighbors. Retry with a different placement.

get.description: Get a single chunk by uid.

delete.description: Delete a chunk. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET. The chunk disappears from listings immediately and stops affecting search after the next re-index pass.

412.description: `If-Match` revision does not match the current chunk

patch.description: Edit a chunk's content / embed / page range. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET.

200.description: Chunk updated. Search results reflect the change after re-indexing completes (`index_status` field).

412.description: `If-Match` revision does not match the current chunk

get.description: List access-shaping rules authored on a knowledge base.

parameters.name: table_name
parameters.description: Restrict to policies that target this exact table.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Restrict to enabled or disabled rules. Omit for both.

parameters.name: include_deleted
parameters.description: When true, also return soft-deleted rules. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Pagination cursor. Pass the previous response's `next_page_token`.

post.description: Author a new access-shaping rule on a knowledge base.

put.description: Enable or disable enforcement of data-governance policies on K2 queries. Requires `k2.policies.manage`. When enabled, queries are rewritten to satisfy active policies or rejected if they cannot be safely constrained. Read the current value from `GET /orgs/{org_id}/k2/{kb_id}` (`governance_enforced` on the KB detail).

get.description: Fetch a single access-shaping rule by id.

delete.description: Soft-delete an access-shaping rule. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET. Assignments remain for audit; the rule no longer applies.

patch.description: Update an access-shaping rule. Requires `If-Match: <revision>` from the prior GET.

get.description: List role bindings for an access-shaping rule.

parameters.name: role_id
parameters.description: Restrict to bindings for this role.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Restrict to enabled or disabled bindings. Omit for both.

parameters.name: include_revoked
parameters.description: When true, include revoked bindings. Defaults to false.

post.description: Bind an access-shaping rule to a role. Re-attaches if the binding was previously revoked.

get.description: Fetch a single role binding for an access-shaping rule.

delete.description: Revoke a role binding (soft). Clears active access without deleting the row.

404.description: Assignment not found or already revoked

post.description: Execute a SQL query against the knowledge base
Drop the knowledge base's in-memory connections so the next request opens a fresh database handle. Requires `k2.manage`.

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

403.description: Caller lacks `k2.manage` or KB not in org

409.description: KB is already under maintenance (no restore row created)

Get the schema for a knowledge base

oneOf.description: Structured schema with tables, columns, indexes, and constraints (default)

requestBody.description: Multipart form with either a `file` field (raw bytes) OR a `url` field (http/https; the server fetches it). Optional `table_name` overrides the auto-derived table name; optional `mode` is `create` (default), `replace`, or `append`.

get.description: Time-binned credits and USD cost breakdown for a project.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Bucket size for aggregation (`minute`, `hour`, or `day`).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive range start (epoch milliseconds). Defaults: 1h / 1d / 30d before `to` by `group_by`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Exclusive range end (epoch milliseconds). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Restrict to a specific user’s runs.

get.description: Time-binned run counts, user counts, and duration percentiles for a project.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Bucket size for aggregation (`minute`, `hour`, or `day`).

get.description: Per-user run and spend aggregates for a project.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive range start (epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Exclusive range end (epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque offset for the next page (from the previous response’s `next_page_token`).

post.description: Send a prompt to the Timbal Composer agent. The `prompt` field accepts a plain string, an array of content blocks, or a full Message object. File blocks (`base64` or `url` source) are staged server-side under the session inbox and replaced with a path-reference text block before reaching the model — bytes never enter the conversation context. The agent can then either `Read` the staged file for inspection, `import_kb_table` it to create queryable DuckDB tables (csv/xlsx/parquet/json), or `add_kb_file` to parse + embed it for semantic/keyword search (pdf/docx/md/txt/audio). Legacy `.xls` is not accepted for tabular ingest; save as `.xlsx` first.

prompt: Load this into the knowledge base as table 'sales' (append mode)

value.prompt: Add error handling to the upload endpoint
How would you refactor the upload endpoint for better error handling?

prompt: Describe what you see in this screenshot and fix the layout

Get a plan's metadata and full markdown body

parameters.name: plan_id
parameters.description: Plan id — the markdown filename without the `.md` extension.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Filter sessions by git branch (worktree revision)

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Session UUID (e.g. "019d1bbe-2f61-7393-afa7-6bb770878c9b")

post.description: Stop a running Composer session by sending SIGTERM to the Claude process

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Session UUID (e.g. "019d1bbe-2f61-7393-afa7-6bb770878c9b")

404.description: No active process for this session

get.description: List environments for a project. Requires `projects.envs.read` on `projects:{project_id}`.

post.description: Create a new environment and a matching git branch from the repo default branch. Requires `projects.envs.create` on `projects:{project_id}`.

400.description: Invalid branch name or project has no repository

409.description: Environment with that name already exists

delete.description: Delete an environment. Requires `projects.envs.delete` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

patch.description: Set or clear the branch for an environment. Requires `projects.envs.branch` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.
Update environment deploy config (CPU, memory, ephemeral storage, replica count). Requires `projects.envs.deploy_config` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

post.description: Deploy an environment (idempotent on the same rev). Requires `projects.envs.deploy` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

400.description: Rev does not exist in the project repo

get.description: List deployments for an environment. Requires `projects.envs.read` on `projects:{project_id}`.

parameters.name: component_id
parameters.description:
Filter to a single workforce component by its numeric app id
(the value the SDK reads from `TIMBAL_APP_ID`, also returned
as `id` on each deployment in the response). Combines with
`component_uid`: when both are set, rows must match both.

parameters.name: component_uid
parameters.description:
Filter to a single workforce component by its manifest uid
(the `_id` field in `timbal.yaml`, also returned as `uid` on
each deployment in the response). Display name is
deliberately not accepted as a filter — it's user-editable
and not guaranteed unique within a project.

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.description:
Optional trigger filter. Accepts:
  * `latest` — return every row sharing the most recent
    trigger for this env; falls back to the latest row per
    component when the env has no trigger history.
  * a UUID — return every row produced by that trigger.

patch.description: Attach, replace, or detach the custom domain mapped to an env. Set `domain_id` to assign or reassign; omit (or send null) to unassign. Requires `projects.envs.domain` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

get.description: Fetch runtime logs for the active deployment of a specific component within an env. `component` selects the component kind (`ui` / `api` / `workforce`); when `component=workforce`, supply exactly one of `component_id` or `component_uid` to identify which one. `workforce` covers agent and workflow components interchangeably. Requires `projects.envs.logs` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`. Always returns the active deployment's logs — a per-deployment override is not exposed yet, as logs from superseded deployments are not retained.

parameters.name: component
parameters.description:
Which component's logs to fetch. Shared vocabulary with
`GET /preview/logs` (which 501s on `workforce`). `workforce`
here covers agent and workflow components interchangeably.

schema.description:
Routing selector for the deployable units a Timbal project owns:
the project-level frontend (`Ui`), the project-level backend (`Api`),
and the N workforce components (`Workforce`, keyed on
`manifest_id`).

Used as a `?component=` query parameter on every surface that acts
on one component at a time (`GET /preview/logs`,
`GET /envs/{env}/logs`, etc.). Wire values are lowercase
(`ui`/`api`/`workforce`). Surfaces that don't apply to a given
variant (e.g. previews don't run workforce components today) reject
with `501 NotImplemented` instead of forking the schema — clients
rely on a single shared vocabulary.

Distinct from [`AppType`], which is a **type discriminator** on
workforce rows (`Agent` vs `Workflow`) carried in response payloads.
`ProjectComponent` collapses both into `Workforce` because every
surface that *selects* a workforce component keys on `manifest_id`,
not on the Agent-vs-Workflow distinction (the underlying log
pipeline, deployment shape, etc. are identical for both). Use
[`From<AppType>`] to bridge: any workforce type — including the
forward-compat `AppType::Unknown` catch-all — maps to
`ProjectComponent::Workforce`.

parameters.name: component_id
parameters.description:
Numeric workforce app id (the value the SDK reads from
`TIMBAL_APP_ID`, also returned as `id` on each deployment in
the deployments-list response). XOR with `component_uid`
when `component=workforce`; forbidden for `ui` / `api`.

parameters.name: component_uid
parameters.description:
Workforce manifest uid (the `_id` field in `timbal.yaml`,
also returned as `uid` on each deployment in the
deployments-list response). XOR with `component_id`.
Display name is deliberately not accepted as a selector —
it's user-editable and not guaranteed unique within a
project.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: ms since epoch. Defaults to `end_time - 1h`.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: ms since epoch. Defaults to now.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description:
Pagination cursor from a prior response. Returned only when
cursor-based pagination is available for the component's
runtime; ignored otherwise.

400.description: Invalid query (workforce without exactly one of component_id/component_uid, or ui/api with either set)

403.description: Insufficient permissions, or no deployment found for the requested component

get.description: Query runtime metrics for an environment. Requires `projects.envs.metrics` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

post.description: Stop an environment. Destructive. Requires `projects.envs.stop` on `projects:{project_id}:envs:{env_id}`.

put.description: Link a K2 knowledge base to a project. Replaces any previously linked KB.

delete.description: Unlink the K2 knowledge base from a project.

get.description: Returns rules scoped to a single **project** (e.g. deployment lifecycle emails). Each rule includes linked channels. Requires `projects.notification_rules.read` on `projects:{project_id}`.

post.description: Create a project-scoped notification rule.

400.description: Validation error (event type, channel config, empty name, etc.)

delete.description: Deletes a rule tied to this project. Requires `projects.notification_rules.manage` on `projects:{project_id}`.

404.description: Rule not found for this project

get.description: Get preview status (starting or running) and health when running

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name of the preview to inspect (for example `main`).

403.description: Caller has no access to the project

404.description: No preview or start in progress for this branch

post.description: Start dev preview servers (API/UI) for a branch worktree; returns starting immediately, then ready after health checks

400.description: Invalid branch or worktree not ready

403.description: Caller is not a project admin

get.description: Tail the last N lines of a running preview component's dev server log (stdout+stderr merged). Admin only. Logs disappear when the preview exits.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name of the preview to read logs from (for example `main`).

parameters.name: component
parameters.description:
Which component to fetch logs for. Preview only runs `ui` and `api`
today; passing `workforce` deserializes successfully but the handler
returns `501 NotImplemented` (the wire vocabulary is shared with
`GET /envs/{env}/logs` so clients can use a single
[`crate::models::ProjectComponent`] across surfaces).

schema.description:
Routing selector for the deployable units a Timbal project owns:
the project-level frontend (`Ui`), the project-level backend (`Api`),
and the N workforce components (`Workforce`, keyed on
`manifest_id`).

Used as a `?component=` query parameter on every surface that acts
on one component at a time (`GET /preview/logs`,
`GET /envs/{env}/logs`, etc.). Wire values are lowercase
(`ui`/`api`/`workforce`). Surfaces that don't apply to a given
variant (e.g. previews don't run workforce components today) reject
with `501 NotImplemented` instead of forking the schema — clients
rely on a single shared vocabulary.

Distinct from [`AppType`], which is a **type discriminator** on
workforce rows (`Agent` vs `Workflow`) carried in response payloads.
`ProjectComponent` collapses both into `Workforce` because every
surface that *selects* a workforce component keys on `manifest_id`,
not on the Agent-vs-Workflow distinction (the underlying log
pipeline, deployment shape, etc. are identical for both). Use
[`From<AppType>`] to bridge: any workforce type — including the
forward-compat `AppType::Unknown` catch-all — maps to
`ProjectComponent::Workforce`.

parameters.name: tail
parameters.description: Max number of lines to return from the end of the log. Default 200, max 10000.

200.description: Last N lines of the log as plain text.

x-log-truncated.description: `true` if the log had more lines than `tail` (or more than ~16 MiB of tail content was skipped); `false` if the full log fits.

403.description: Caller is not a project admin

404.description: No running preview for this branch/component, or log file missing

post.description: Stop preview dev servers for a branch

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

403.description: Caller is not a project admin

404.description: No preview running for this branch

get.description: List runs for a project. Org members see their own runs; `projects.runs.list` is required to list all runs or filter by another user.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter runs by user ID

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter runs by status

parameters.name: sentiment
parameters.description: Filter runs by sentiment

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Filter runs by group ID

parameters.name: workforce_id
parameters.description: Filter runs by workforce ID

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description:
Filter runs by the git branch they were executed against (e.g. `main`).
Runs without a recorded branch are always included for backwards
compatibility with rows created before this field was tracked.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Filter runs by start time (epoch timestamp in milliseconds)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Filter runs by end time (epoch timestamp in milliseconds)

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: Filter runs by metadata

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Page token

get.description: Get a single run for a project (by run id or idempotency key). Returned when you initiated the run, or when you hold `projects.runs.list` for the project.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Platform run id or idempotency key.

get.description: List reactions for a project run. Returned when you initiated the run, or when you hold `projects.runs.list` for the project.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Platform run id or idempotency key.

post.description: Add a reaction to a project run. Allowed when the caller initiated the run, or when the caller holds `projects.runs.react` for the project.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Platform run id or idempotency key.

get.description: List variables for a project. Requires `projects.vars.read` on `projects:{project_id}`.

parameters.name: env_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of environment IDs to filter by

post.description: Create a new variable. Requires `projects.vars.manage` on `projects:{project_id}`.
Batch create variables. Requires `projects.vars.manage` on `projects:{project_id}`.

get.description: Get a variable by ID. Requires `projects.vars.read` on `projects:{project_id}`.

delete.description: Delete a variable. Requires `projects.vars.manage` on `projects:{project_id}`.

get.description: List workforce components on the git branch given by `rev`.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

post.description: Create a workforce component (agent or workflow) on the git **branch** given by `rev`.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

delete.description: Delete a workforce component on the git **branch** given by `rev`.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

put.description: Link an existing ACE to a workforce component. Replaces any previously linked ACE.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Organization id

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Project id

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

post.description: Create a new ACE attached to a workforce component and enqueue the initial generation job, seeded from the component's current source on the given branch. Returns `202` immediately with `{ace_uid, job_uid}`; poll `GET /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}` for terminal state. The ACE's name defaults to `{project_name}/{workforce_name}`.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Organization id

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Project id

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

delete.description: Detach the ACE currently attached to a workforce component. The ACE itself is preserved and can be reattached to any workforce component. Active jobs on the ACE are NOT cancelled — use `POST /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}/cancel` if that's what you want.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Organization id

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Project id

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

schema.description: Successful responses use HTTP 204 and do not include a body.

get.description: Proxy HTTP to a **running** workforce deployment (agent or workflow). Non-serverless: method and headers pass through; JSON on `/run` or `/stream` may get `context.platform_config` merged for SDK auth-back. Serverless: no upstream URL — the platform runs the workforce for this request. Pass `workforce` as id, manifest UUID, or name; `rev` is the git branch. Response header `X-Timbal-Workforce-Deployment-Type` is `serverless`, `ecs`, or `on-premise`. WebSocket upgrades are not supported.

parameters.name: org_id
parameters.description: Organization id

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Project id

parameters.name: workforce
parameters.description: Workforce component: numeric id, manifest UUID, or name

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path on the deployment server after the component segment (for example `run` or `healthcheck`). Leave empty to call the deployment root.

parameters.name: rev
parameters.description: Git branch name (for example `main`).

404.description: No running deployment found for this component on this branch

502.description: The deployment was unreachable, dropped the connection, or returned an invalid response

504.description: The deployment did not respond within the proxy timeout

get.description: Platform starter catalog for the CLI and create flows.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by template type (`ui`, `api`, `agent`, `workflow`, `project`).

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Filter by slug (e.g. `ui-simple-chat`).

AceJobView.description: Public view of a background job against an ACE.

error_message.description: Present when the job terminated unsuccessfully (`failed`, `error`, `cancelled`).

AcePolicy.description:
A single policy entry.

- `id` is the human-readable stable key (e.g. `"ev_subsidy"`).
- `requires` / `provides` / `tool_action` are kept as raw JSON because
  their shape is still evolving.

AceVar.description:
A single variable declaration.

- `id` is the human-readable stable key (e.g. `"flow"`, `"presupuesto_max"`).
- `choices`, when present, restricts the variable to an enum of string values.
- `match_mode` tweaks how the matcher compares extracted vs expected values
  (e.g. `"semantic"`).

description.description: One-sentence description for tooltips / role-builder copy.

key.description:
Stable action key — pass as `RoleGrants.action` when authoring a
custom role.

label.description: Short, human-readable name for UI display.

oneOf.description:
`null` for actions that don't target a sub-resource (e.g.
`users.read`, `billing.manage`); `RoleGrants.resource` MUST be
`null` for these. Otherwise the resource shape spec.

kind.description:
Stable wire name: `project`, `project_env`, `integration`, etc.
`integration`. Discriminator the UI may key off when it wants a
specific picker variant.

segments.description:
Ordered list of segment-kind names (e.g. `["projects", "envs"]`).
`segments.len()` is the number of `{id}` slots in `template`.

template.description:
Canonical example path with `{id}` placeholders (e.g.
`"projects:{id}:envs:{id}"`). Per-segment `*` wildcards are
accepted by the evaluator.

AppType.description:
Read/output enum for a workforce component's type.

Tolerant on deserialization (`#[serde(other)]`) because this type is reconstructed
from DB rows via two paths that both must not fail on unknown values:
  1. [`AppType::try_from`] — used by `FromRow` impls reading `OrgsApps.type` directly.
  2. `serde_json::from_value` — used by handlers that build workforce JSON via
     `json_build_object('type', oa.type, ...)` (runs list, deployments, etc.) and
     then deserialize into `WorkforcePreview` / `RunPreview`.

New workforce types written by `on_push` (or older ones removed from this enum) must
not break list/get endpoints, hence `Unknown` as a catch-all.

**Do not use this for API request bodies** — see [`AppTypeInput`] for the strict
input variant that rejects unknowns at the deserialization boundary.

AttachmentReq.description:
A single (role, scope) attachment in the desired state.

`scope` is an optional hierarchical resource path (`projects:42`,
`projects:42:envs:5`). `null` / omitted = unscoped (the role's grants
apply as-written). The same role may appear multiple times with
different scopes — the unique key on `OrgUserRoles` is
`(org, user, role, scope) NULLS NOT DISTINCT`.

oneOf.description: Replace the column value with a SQL literal expression (e.g. `'***'`).
Non-keyed hash. Use `pseudonymize` for stronger guarantees.
HMAC-keyed deterministic pseudonym. Stable across queries that share `key_ref`.
Truncate a string column to `length` characters, optionally pad-suffixed.
Format-preserving redaction of a payment account number.
Email with the local part hidden (`***@example.com`).
Generalize a date or timestamp to the start of the given unit.
Cap a numeric column at a maximum value.

additionalProperties.description:
Per-column transformation applied to query results when this rule matches.

The full vocabulary is reserved at the API boundary so future deployments
can add masks without breaking existing clients. Only `redact`, `hash`,
and `truncate` are accepted by the current release; the others return
`400` until enforcement ships.

cap_credits.description: Max overage **credits** the user allows (null = unlimited).

Budget.description: Credit budget on a project (shared pool) or role (per attached user).

BudgetPatchBody.description: Partial budget update — merge rows without touching other configured targets.

BudgetPatchInput.description: One budget change in a PATCH merge. `limit_credits: null` removes the budget.

limit_credits.description: When null, removes the budget for this target. When set, must be > 0.

ChannelAction.description: How the rule was wired to `NotificationChannels` for this create call.

action.description:
Action key (e.g. `projects.envs.deploy`). Discover keys via
`GET /orgs/{org_id}/iam/actions`. Unknown keys → 422.

resource.description:
Concrete resource path for actions whose `resource.kind != "none"`
(e.g. `projects:7:envs:3`). MUST be omitted / null for `none`-kind
actions. Wildcards (`*`) are rejected — the simulator answers for
a concrete target, not a pattern.

checks.description:
`(action, resource?)` tuples to evaluate. Capped at 50. An empty
list is rejected — almost always a client bug.

user_id.description:
Target user to evaluate. Defaults to the caller. Passing another
user's id requires the `users.read` grant on this org.

results.description: One result per input check, in input order.

user_id.description: Echoes the evaluated user id (input or caller).

matched_allows.description: Grants that matched as `allow`. Empty for `no_allow` / `superadmin`.

matched_denies.description: Grants that matched as `deny`. Non-empty only for `deny_overrides`.

CloudWatchLogEvent.description: A single log event from CloudWatch Logs.

log_stream.description: The log stream name this event came from.

ComposeEffort.description: Reasoning effort level for the session.

ComposeModel.description: Model family to use for the session. Always resolves to the latest released version.

oneOf.description:
Reasoning effort level. Higher levels let the model think longer before responding,
at proportionally higher cost and latency.

Session behavior mode. `default` bypasses all tool-permission prompts and lets the agent
edit files, run shell commands, and call MCP tools freely. `plan` restricts the agent to
read-only tools — it analyses the codebase and produces a plan, without mutating anything.

Model family to use. Always resolves to the latest version of that family —
specific version pinning is not exposed.

prompt.description: The prompt: a plain string, an array of content blocks, or a full Message object.

rev.description: Branch to run against (worktree is checked out at this rev)

session_id.description: Existing session ID to continue a conversation. Omit to start a new session.

dry_run.description: When omitted or when the request has no JSON body, treated as `false`.

config.description: Omitted or null when linking by `id` only.

id.description: Existing `NotificationChannels.id` for this org. When set, `type` / `config` / `name` are ignored.

enabled.description: Defaults to true; set false to author the rule in a draft state.

row_filter_dsl.description: Row predicate JSON. Stored opaquely in this release.

table_name.description: Target table within the knowledge base. Omit to apply to every table.

name.description: Name for the new knowledge base

directory.description: Folder path, same format as the `directory` field on file upload (e.g. `docs/reports`).

created.description: `true` if this call created the folder, `false` if it already existed.

placeholder_file_id.description: Listing id for this folder; remove it with `DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/k2/{kb_id}/files/{file_id}`.

CreateNotificationChannelReq.description: Body for **POST** `/orgs/{org_id}/notifications/channels` (standalone channel; same validation as inline rule channel, no `id`).

CreateNotificationRuleReq.description: Body for POST org-wide or project-scoped notification rules (same shape).

channel.description: Channel: inline `type` + `config`, or reuse with `{ "id": <channel id> }` (same org).

grants.description:
Grants this role carries. Empty list is allowed (creates a no-op
role; admin can extend it later).

name.description:
Role name. Must be unique within the org. Reserved system names
(`admin`, `developer`, `billing`, `member`, `user`) are rejected.
Optional display name for the new component. If omitted, a unique name is generated.

CreateWorkforceResponse.description: Response returned when a new workforce component is created.

path.description: Path to the new component inside the repository worktree (for example `workforce/my-agent`).

uid.description: Identifier assigned in the project manifest for this component.

CreditGrant.description: A single credit grant on an organization.

amount_credits.description:
Credits awarded by this grant. Always positive; corrections ship as a
separate grant rather than a negative amount.

consumed_credits.description:
Credits already drawn from this grant by attributed costs. Bounded by
`amount_credits`; remaining headroom is `amount_credits - consumed_credits`.

exhausted_at.description:
Set once the grant has been fully consumed by usage; `null` while
the grant still has unspent credits or hasn't been reconciled yet.

expires_at.description: When the grant stops being spendable. `null` = never expires.

granted_by_user_id.description: User who triggered the grant. `null` for system-issued grants.

starts_at.description: When the grant became (or will become) spendable.

uid.description: Stable public identifier for this grant.

force.description: When `true`, delete even if rules reference this channel (cascading unlink).

workforce.description: Component to delete: numeric id, manifest UUID (`uid`), or configured name.

DeploymentDetail.description:
Public-API representation of a single deployment row, returned by the
env-scoped deployments list endpoint. Flattens the runtime config
(cpu/memory/...) inline rather than nesting under `config` — frontend
consumes the fields directly.

DeploymentTarget.description:
Image/version pointer embedded in [`DeploymentDetail::target`]. Distinct
from [`crate::deployments::models::DeployTarget`] (the domain-layer spec
carrying image URIs / platform). This shape is the public-API view of
what a deployment is *of*.

project_env.description: Project environment this domain is linked to, if any

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp in epoch milliseconds

validation_records.description: DNS records required for SSL certificate validation

auto_processing_enabled.description: Toggle the automatic parsing, chunking, and embedding pipeline for new file uploads. When false, uploads are stored as-is. Existing files are not affected.

banner_url.description: URL of the organization's banner image

photo_url.description: URL of the organization's profile photo

grants.description: Full replacement of the role's grant set.

name.description: New role name. Reserved system names rejected.

resource.description:
Scope-intersected resource — the pattern the live evaluator would
match against. `null` for `none`-kind actions.

via.description:
Attribution: which role + which attachment scope + the original
grant resource (pre-intersection).

grants.description:
One row per `(attachment × grant)` pair, ordered for stable
rendering. May contain duplicates on `(effect, action, resource)`
when multiple roles contribute the same grant — `via` distinguishes
them.

is_superadmin.description:
`true` if the target is a platform superadmin. RBAC is bypassed
for every action regardless of `grants[]` (which will be empty
unless a superadmin happens to also have org role attachments).

EnqueueJobResBody.description:
Response body returned by any endpoint that enqueues a new job. The
HTTP status is `202 Accepted` — poll `GET /ace/{ace_uid}/jobs/{job_uid}`
for terminal state.

last_deployment_status.description: Status of the latest deployment in this environment

media_type.description: MIME type. Inferred from `name` extension when omitted.

name.description:
Filename (e.g. "screenshot.png"). Used for the staged file name on disk
and for media-type inference. Optional — when omitted, `media_type` must
be provided explicitly and a generic name is generated.

source.description: How to obtain the file bytes.

GrantAttribution.description:
Attribution for a single grant: which role contributed it, what
attachment-level scope was in effect, and the resource pattern as
authored on the role (pre-scope-intersection).

Shared by `POST /iam/check` (matched_allows / matched_denies) and
`GET /iam/users/{id}/effective-grants` (the `via` field on each row).

To derive the effective resource a grant matched against, compute
`resource_pattern_intersect(grant_resource, attachment_scope)`. When
`attachment_scope` is `null`, the effective resource equals
`grant_resource`.

GrantEffect.description: Effect of a [`RoleGrant`]. `deny` wins at eval time.

after_uid.description:
Place the new chunk immediately after this `chunk_uid`. Mutually
exclusive with `before_uid`. If neither is set, the chunk is
appended at the end of the file.

before_uid.description:
Place the new chunk immediately before this `chunk_uid`. Mutually
exclusive with `after_uid`.

embed.description:
Text used for vector / full-text indexing. Defaults to `content`
when omitted; provide an explicit value to keep an
indexing-optimized variant distinct from the user-visible text.

emails.description: Email addresses to invite to the organization.

send_email.description: Whether to send an invitation email to each invitee. Defaults to `true`.

blocks.description:
Source-document layout metadata (e.g. bounding boxes, page coords)
when available. `null` for chunks created or edited by the user.

index_status.description:
Indexing state. One of `pending`, `indexing`, `indexed`, `failed`.
While not `indexed`, the chunk's contribution to search results may
be stale.

ordinal.description:
Ordering key. Chunks in a file are returned in ascending `ordinal`
order. Treat the value as opaque — placement on insert is controlled
by `after_uid` / `before_uid`, not by setting `ordinal` directly.

revision.description:
Optimistic concurrency token. Send back as `If-Match: <revision>`
on the next mutation (`PATCH` / `DELETE`).

K2ChunkSummary.description:
List-endpoint payload. Same fields as [`K2Chunk`] except `blocks`,
which is omitted to keep list responses small. Fetch a single chunk
by `uid` to retrieve `blocks`.

columns.description: Columns involved (for PK, UNIQUE, NOT_NULL). Empty for CHECK.

auto_processing_enabled.description: When false, file uploads to this knowledge base are stored as-is and skipped by the automatic parsing, chunking, and embedding pipeline.

governance_enforced.description: When true, K2 queries are enforced against active governance policies (rewritten or rejected). When false, governance is audit-only. Toggle via `PUT …/k2/{kb_id}/policies/enforcement` (`k2.policies.manage`).

parse_state.description:
Pipeline state for parsing + embedding.

One of `pending` (pipeline in flight), `success` (parse + embed both completed),
`failed` (latest attempt failed), or `skipped` (format unsupported, never parsed).
Unsupported files are stored as-is; the indicator lets clients flag them in listings
rather than rejecting the upload.

K2GovernanceEnforcementResponse.description: Whether governance policies are enforced on queries (vs audit-only).

break_glass_allowed.description: Whether sessions may bypass the rule via a logged escalation.

oneOf.description:
Per-column transformation map. Null means no masks.
`{"col": {"type":"redact"}}` is the canonical "hide this column".

enabled.description: When false, the rule body is inactive everywhere it is bound.

revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token. Echo back via `If-Match` on mutations.

row_filter_dsl.description: Row predicate authored as JSON. Stored opaquely in this release.

table_name.description: Target table within the knowledge base. Null applies to every table.

K2PolicyAuditLevel.description: Audit verbosity for matches against this rule.

K2PolicyCombineMode.description: Postgres-RLS-style combination semantic for a policy binding.

statements.description: DDL statements (CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, etc.)

LinkAceReqBody.description: Request body for linking an existing ACE to a workforce.

ace_uid.description: `uid` of an existing ACE in the same organization.

kb_id.description: ID of the K2 knowledge base to link to this project

actions.description:
Every action this org may grant on a role, in catalog order. Group
on the client by the first dot-segment of `key` (e.g. `users.*` →
"Users", `notification_channels.*` → "Notification channels").

ListOrgNotificationRulesResBody.title: ListOrgNotificationRulesResponse
ListOrgNotificationRulesResBody.description: Wrapper for **GET** `/orgs/{org_id}/notifications/rules` — org-scoped rules only (`project_id` is null in storage).

roles.description:
Roles available to this organization — system rows first, then
custom rows alphabetically by name.

next_page_token.description: Token to fetch the next page, or null if this is the last page

org_users.description: List of users in the organization

ListPreviewsResponse.description: Previews currently visible for the organization.

ListWorkforceResBody.description: Workforce components on the git branch given by `rev`. The `url` field is set only when the component has a **running** deployment for that branch.

billing_suspended.description:
`true` when billing is suspended because a finalized invoice charge
failed and is still unpaid. While suspended, credit-consuming requests
are blocked until the outstanding invoice is paid.

plan_cancel_at.description:
Unix timestamp (seconds) of the scheduled plan cancellation; `null` when
no cancellation is pending.

plan_cancel_at_period_end.description:
`true` between the moment a user schedules a plan cancellation in the
Billing Portal and the final cancellation taking effect.

title.description: Optional job title (e.g. "Senior AI Solutions Architect").

type.description: Role this contact plays for the org.

primary.description: Marks the preferred channel of this kind when multiple are listed.

value.description:
Channel-specific identifier: email address, E.164 phone number, slack
handle, calendly URL, etc. Format is enforced by the channel kind.

OrgContactType.description:
Role a contact plays for the org. Free-form on the wire/DB so new roles
can be introduced without a migration; unknown values fall back to `Other`.

banner_url.description: URL of the organization's banner image

n_projects.description: Number of projects in the organization

photo_url.description: URL of the organization's profile photo

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the organization

id.description: Unique user identifier. Null for pending invites that haven't been accepted yet

photo_url.description: URL of the user's profile photo

role.description:
Legacy single-role text. Kept for backward compatibility; new
integrations should rely on `roles`.

roles.description:
Roles currently attached to this membership. Empty for pending
invites and for members with no role attachments.

embed.description:
Text used for vector / full-text indexing. When `content` is
provided and `embed` is omitted, `embed` is auto-set to the new
`content`. Send an explicit value to keep an indexing-optimized
variant distinct from the user-visible text.

page_start.description:
Tri-state: omit to leave unchanged, send `null` to clear, send an
integer to set.

oneOf.description: Omit to leave unchanged; send `null` to clear all masks.

description.description: Omit to leave unchanged; send `null` to clear.

row_filter_dsl.description: Omit to leave unchanged; send `null` to clear the row filter.

table_name.description: Omit to leave unchanged; send `null` to apply to every table.

access_request_data.description:
Free-form access-request metadata. Merged (shallow) into the existing
stored object server-side.

name.description: Display name. 1–64 characters. Cannot be set to null.

phone.description: Phone number. Pass null to clear.

photo_url.description: Profile photo URL. Pass null to clear.

content.description: Raw markdown body of the plan file.

PlanDetail.description: A plan markdown file plus its full body.

component.description: Which part of the preview this row describes: `api` (backend dev server) or `ui` (frontend dev server).

healthy.description: Whether the dev server answered a health check on its port. Absent when no check was performed.

project_id.description: Included on organization-wide preview listings so you can tell which project a row belongs to.

rev.description: Git branch name this preview is tied to.

slug.description: Opaque token used with your organization’s preview hostname.

started_at.description: UTC timestamp when acceptance of the preview start was recorded.

url.description: Public HTTPS URL for the running preview, when preview hosting is configured for your organization.

default_role.description: Project's default role. All organization members will be assigned this role by default for this project

environments.description: Project environments with their latest deployment status

has_ui.description: Whether the project has a UI interface

is_public_template.description: Whether the project is a public template

knowledge_bases.description: Knowledge bases connected to this project (new KB system only)

publishable_api_key.description: Publishable API key for this project

repository_url.description: URL of the project's source code repository. When null, the project does not have source code available

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the project

screenshot_url.description: URL of the project's screenshot image

template_uses.description: Number of times this template has been used

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp in epoch milliseconds

use_platform_iam.description: Whether the project uses the platform's IAM system for access control

agents.description:
Agent directory names to scaffold under `workforce/`. Omit for an
empty workforce directory.

kb_id.description:
Knowledge base to attach to the project. Provide an existing KB's
id to link it, or omit to auto-create an empty one.

with_ui.description:
Include the default web UI scaffold. Requires at least one entry in
`agents` (workflows alone do not use the web UI).

workflows.description: Workflow directory names to scaffold under `workforce/`.

ProjectPlan.description:
A plan markdown file produced by a compose `plan` mode session. Plans live
on EFS at `{EFS}/orgs/{org_id}/projects/{project_id}/.claude/plans/*.md`
and are authored by the Claude CLI — the API only reads them.

preview.description: Short body preview (first non-empty paragraph after the title, trimmed).

title.description: Title parsed from the first `# ` heading (with an optional `Plan: ` prefix stripped).

default_role.description: Project's default role. All organization members will be assigned this role by default for this project

is_public_template.description: Whether the project is a public template

role.description: Authenticated user's role in the project

screenshot_url.description: URL of the project's screenshot image

template_uses.description: Number of times this template has been used

first_prompt.description: Truncated text of the first user prompt — used as a session title.

rev.description: Git branch the session is scoped to

tool_call_count.description: Total tool_use blocks across all calls

explain.description: When true, runs EXPLAIN ANALYZE and includes the query plan in the response.

params.description:
Optional parameters for the query. Parameters passed to the vector
search functions (`lance_vector_search`, `lance_hybrid_search`) are
automatically converted to embedding vectors before the query runs.

sql.description:
SQL query to execute against the knowledge base.
Use $1, $2, etc. for parameter placeholders.

email.description: Email address of the user to remove from the organization

attachments.description:
Desired attachment set for this member. Attachments present today
but not in the list are detached; new ones are attached. An empty
list detaches everything. Duplicate `(role_id, scope)` pairs are
ignored.

name.description: Display name (max 64 characters, unique per user).

org_id.description:
Organization to bind the credential to. Set alone for an org-only key
(full access within that org); required when `scope` is set. Omit with
`scope` for a full-access key across all your orgs.

scope.description:
IAM scope path (e.g. `projects:42`) restricting the credential to one
resource subtree. Requires `org_id`. Omit for an org-only or full-access key.

Response.description:
Resolved window echoed back so the client can paginate / refresh
without re-deriving the defaults the server applied.

RoleDetail.description: A role with its full grant set.

grants.description: Domain grants this role carries. May be empty.

RoleGrant.description:
A single grant on a role — `(effect, action, resource?, condition?)`.

`resource` is `None` for `ResourceKind::None` actions (`users.read`),
or a hierarchical path like `projects:42:envs:5` (or `projects:42:envs:*`)
for typed actions.

`condition` is opaque JSON reserved for future ABAC; ignored by the
evaluator in v1, accepted and stored as-is by writes.

RoleInUseConflict.description:
Returned when `DELETE /orgs/{org_id}/iam/roles/{role_id}` is called while
the role is still attached to one or more members.

user_ids.description: Members currently holding this role via `OrgUserRoles`.

RolePreview.description:
Compact role record returned by listing endpoints. Fetch a single
role to retrieve the full grant set.

name.description:
Role name. Unique per `(org_id)` for custom roles, globally unique
for system roles.

RoleSource.description:
Whether a role is a built-in preset or defined by the organization.
`system` roles are read-only; `custom` roles can be edited or deleted
by an organization admin.

messages.description: Message history from the Claude session file (same format as compose stream events)

name.description: When null, the branch will be cleared

governance_enforced.description:
When true, queries are rewritten to satisfy active policies or rejected
if they cannot be safely constrained. When false, governance is audit-only.

force.description:
If true and the org preview concurrency cap is reached, evict the
oldest running (or in-flight) preview in the org to free a slot
instead of returning 429. Defaults to false.

rev.description: Git branch name to run the preview for (for example `main`).
Git branch name you used when starting the preview (for example `main`).

TempFile.description:
Stateless temporary file metadata. Stored under a dedicated `tmp/` prefix
in object storage and removed by storage lifecycle policy ~24h after upload.
No database row is created; clients must persist the URL/name themselves
if they need to track it.

content_length.description: Size of the uploaded payload in bytes.

content_type.description: MIME type as reported by the client.

expires_at.description:
Advisory expiration (~24h after upload). Object deletion is performed by
storage lifecycle and may lag this timestamp by up to a day. Treat HTTP
404 from `url` as "expired".

name.description: Original filename (sanitized — slashes / control characters replaced).

url.description: Public URL where the file can be downloaded until it expires.

github_ref.description: Git branch or tag name used when building `tarball_url`.

github_repo_full_name.description: GitHub `owner/repo` for blueprint templates. Omitted for embedded scaffolds.

is_default.description: When true, this row is the default pick for its `type` within the catalog scope.

slug.description: Stable identifier for CLI and UI selection (e.g. `ui-simple-chat`).

tarball_url.description: GitHub archive tarball URL derived from `github_repo_full_name` and `github_ref`.

Token.description: User credential (API key or future kinds).

hint.description: Masked secret for display (prefix and suffix only).

org_id.description: Organization the credential is scoped to. Omitted for legacy full-access keys.

scope.description: IAM scope path (e.g. `projects:42`). Omitted for legacy full-access keys.

secret.description: Full secret value. Present only in the create response; omitted on list.

deleted_at.description: Set when the workforce component has been deleted. Only returned from endpoints that include soft-deleted rows (e.g. runs list).

id.description: Identifier of this workforce component within the project.

type.description: Component category (for example agent or workflow).

uid.description: Stable identifier from the project manifest (`timbal.yaml`), when defined.

url.description: When present, the authenticated Timbal API URL used to call this component’s **running** deployment (for example from the list-workforce response). Omitted where the response does not expose a callable URL, such as on project detail.

bearer_auth.description: Timbal API key. Obtain your API key from the Timbal platform settings. See [Authentication](/api-reference/authentication) for more information.

tags.name: iam
tags.description: IAM — actions, roles, users, and authorization introspection

tags.name: preview
tags.description: Project dev previews (branch worktree dev servers)
